title: verbascum carmanicum subsp. carmanicum
intro: Verbascum carmanicum subsp. carmanicum, also known as Carman's mullein, is a perennial plant species in the figwort family native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties, as well as to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The herbal preparations of Verbascum carmanicum subsp. carmanicum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used internally or externally to harness its medicinal benefits.
